glosses: godly people fully devoted to their Lord
angel Michael, who is in charge of provisions
angel Malik, the Keeper of Hell
Satan, the Devil; devil; literally to be far away from Allah’s mercy
Iblis, Satan’s name before his fall from grace; from b-l-s, to lose hope
an evil human or jinn associate
an angel that accompanies a person
the Trumpet which will be blown, signalling the beginning of Judgment Day
the Striking Disaster, one of the names of Judgment Day
the Inevitable Hour, one of the names of Judgment Day
the Deafening Blast, one of the names of Judgment Day
the Supreme Disaster, one of the names of Judgment Day
the Day of mutual loss and gain, one of the names of Judgment Day
the Overwhelming Event, one of the names of Judgment Day
the Inevitable Event, one of the names of Judgment Day
Paradise or a Garden in Paradise
a drink; literally a glass of wholesome wine
ripe fruit; literally fruit ready for picking
Salsabil is the name of a spring in Paradise
Tasnim is the name of a spring in Paradise
fresh; literally water that is not unfresh
fire, a source of heat and light
shackles that prevent one from moving
deterring punishment/deterrence that prevents others from committing the same crime
Saqar, one of the names of Hell.
The name of a horrible tree that grows from the depths of Hell.
Abu Lahab, father of the flames
Columns, from the sense of branching off.
A lowly, thorny shrub eaten by the people of Hell.
A breath, wind blow, or whiff.
Kaʿbah; the Sacred House in Makkah
Yathrib, the pre-Islamic name for Madinah
oaths (taken with one’s right hand raised or while shaking with the right hand)
at that time, for a while
what is ahead, in front of
Israel, another name for Prophet Jacob
Ahmad, another name for Prophet Muhammad
the Man in the Whale (Prophet Yunus)
Pharaoh, title of the ruler of Egypt in the story of Prophet Musa.
Mary, mother of Prophet Isa; the only woman mentioned by name in the Quran.
Korah, a wealthy Israelite in Egypt who acted arrogantly and was destroyed.
Goliath, a giant warrior killed by young Dawud.
The Samiri, the man from Samaria who led the Children of Israel into calf-worship.
Imran, father of Mary, the mother of Isa.
A righteous king who toured the earth in service of Allah.
Saul, a righteous man appointed king over the Children of Israel after Musa.
An African man known for wisdom around the time of Prophet Dawud.
Father of Prophet Ibrahim, who rejected the message, made idols, and died as a pagan.
Abu Lahab, uncle of Prophet Muhammad who opposed Islam in Makkah and died as a pagan.
The only companion of Prophet Muhammad mentioned by name in the Quran.
Ezra, one of the most important scholars in Jewish history.
Thamud, the people of Prophet Salih.
Ad, the people of Prophet Hud.
Midian, the people of Prophet Shuayb.
The residents of the Forest, the people of Prophet Shuayb.
The people of Tubba; Tubba Al-Himyari was an ancient righteous Yemeni king.
A pagan people named after their water-pit, to whom Allah sent Prophet Shuayb.
Sheba, an ancient city in Yemen.
Gog, one of two wild groups sealed behind a barrier built by Zul-Qarnayn.
Iram, the people of Ad to whom Prophet Hud was sent.
The residents of the Stone Valley, also known as Thamud.
Pagan people who dug a ditch and filled it with fire to burn Christians of Najran.
the people of Quraysh, the tribe of the Prophet, in charge of maintaining the Kaabah
Islam, full submission to the Will of Allah.
Faith, religion, or way of life.
Path or way, usually followed by mustaqim (straight).
The Sabians, a small indigenous group in Iraq believing in a supreme being.
The natural way; from the sense of giving a distinctive flavour.
A clear way of faith; literally a path to water.
The Quran, revealed to Prophet Muhammad; literally recitation.
The Torah, original revelation given to Prophet Musa.
The Gospel, original revelation given to Prophet Isa.
Psalms, the original revelation given to Prophet Dawud.
Mosque, a place where worshippers bow down.
Sanctuary, where one fights against Satan and evil desires.
Polytheism, associating others with Allah in worship.
Idols, statues of humans or animals used as objects of worship.
Objects of worship without human or animal form.
Drawing lots, a pagan decision-making practice using arrows.
One of five righteous people later worshipped by the people of Nuh.
One of five idols worshipped by the people of Nuh.
One of five idols worshipped by the people of Nuh.
One of five idols worshipped by the people of Nuh.
One of five idols worshipped by the people of Nuh.
One of three major idols worshipped by Arab pagans.
One of three major idols worshipped by Arab pagans.
One of three major idols worshipped by Arab pagans.
An idol worshipped by the people of Prophet Ilyas.
Infant girls buried alive for fear of poverty or shame.
Those witches casting spells by blowing on knots.
A camel custom in which camels were allowed to pasture freely after births; the bahirah had split ears.
A camel left to roam freely.
A camel custom tied to continuous female births.
A protected camel that fathered ten camels.
Those who cheat on the scales.
A person, mostly referring to a man.
Mother, literally or metaphorically: centre, foundation, or home.
brothers of one’s mother / maternal uncles
sisters of one’s mother / maternal aunts
brothers of one’s father / paternal uncles
sisters of one’s father / paternal aunts
state of one who dies without leaving children or parents as heirs
clan from which one branched out
age of puberty and maturity, signified by wet dreams
the Most Forbearing, one of Allah’s Names
young boy; young servants in plural
a baby as a clinging substance inside the mother
a wife left suspended, neither fully married nor divorced
hand and idiomatic extensions involving front, agency, regret, and cutting hands
men (who are always on their feet, providing for their families)
face and idiomatic meanings such as pleasure, first part of day, and Allah’s Face

neck in the idiom of freeing a slave
unbearable / distressed, literally too big to get the arms around or carry

spiritual sickness / hypocrisy, describing one's heart

(fmn.) the one that has something or a certain quality

mirage (which moves about in the heat of the desert)

the state of being overwhelmed, entrenched in

illegitimate child, born out of wedlock
the Sustainer, one of Allah’s Names

(lit., for daylight to shine after dawn—hence, to express oneself clearly) more eloquent

(lit., to blanket) overwhelming people with persistent demands

protected in one's custody (lit., in your laps)

trained to hunt like a dog
horses so well-trained they stand on three legs with one front hoof just touching the ground
digested matter inside the belly of an animal; filth

joined, clustered, stemming from the same root
